Git

GitHub

GitHub (Leads)

HTML, CSS, JavaScript

Frontend (React)

Backend (Node)

Full Stack (Django)

Data Science

Computing

Stuck? Check these out!

We've created some tutorials to help you learn more about each topic and guide you in the right direction. Check them out below!

1. Git/GitHub

Any project that involves collaboration, version control, or sharing code with others would benefit from learning Git and GitHub.

2. HTML/CSS/JavaScript

Any project that involves building a website or web application would benefit from learning HTML, CSS, and JavaScript.

3. Frontend (React)

Any project that involves building a dynamic and interactive user interface would benefit from learning React. This could include web applications that require real-time updates, dashboards that display complex data, or interactive tools that require user input.

4. Backend (Node)

Any project that involves building a server-side application or API would benefit from learning Node.js. This could include web applications that require authentication, database integration, or complex business logic.

5. Full Stack (Django)

Any project that involves building a full-stack web application would benefit from learning Django.

6. Data Science

Any project that involves working with data would benefit from learning data science.

7. Computing

Any project that involves large-scale data processing or machine learning would benefit from learning computing. By harnessing the power of modern computing technologies such as CPUs, GPUs, and specialized hardware, you can tackle complex problems and unlock new insights from your data.

8. Design

Any project that involves creating a visual or interactive experience for users would benefit from learning design principles. This could include websites, mobile apps, desktop apps, video games, and more.

Git

Git is a version-control system that you will use to update your team's code. This tutorial will cover most of the necessary functions. If you need a different function or a more thorough explanation, you will find it online.

Prerequisites

  • Terminal proficiency
  • Git installed
    • Windows: https://gitforwindows.org/
    • Mac: https://sourceforge.net/projects/git-osx-installer/files/

To make sure you have git installed, run the following command in your terminal. It should return a version number if that's true.

git --version

For the full Git tutorial with images, please refer to our comprehensive documentation.

Basic Git Functions

git init

Initialize a git repository

git status

View the status of changed files

git add <filename>

Stage files for commit (use . for all files)

git commit -m "your message"

Commit your staged changes

git log

View commit history

Branches

git branch

List all branches

git branch <name>

Create a new branch

git checkout <name>

Switch to a branch

Push/Pull

git clone <url>

Clone a repository

git pull

Get the latest changes

git push

Push your changes to remote

GitHub

This tutorial is meant for team members to set up their device in order to contribute code to their project.

Prerequisites

  • Github account: https://github.com/
  • Git installed + proficiency
  • Text editor (VScode): https://code.visualstudio.com/download
  • An email invite from project lead via Github

Setup Steps

  1. Check your email with an invite from your project lead
  2. Accept the invitation to collaborate
  3. Copy the repository URL
  4. Clone the repository: git clone <URL>
  5. Create a new branch and start working

Sending Your Changes to GitHub

git push --set-upstream origin <branch-name>

After the first push, you can simply use git push

Create a pull request on GitHub to merge your changes to the main branch.

GitHub for Project Leads

This section covers setting up repositories and managing team access for project leads.

Setting Up a Repository

  1. Create a new repository on GitHub
  2. Add branch protection rules for the main branch
  3. Invite team members as collaborators
  4. Set up required reviews for pull requests

HTML, CSS, JavaScript

The foundational technologies for web development.

HTML (HyperText Markup Language)

HTML provides the structure of web pages. Learn more at W3Schools HTML

CSS (Cascading Style Sheets)

CSS handles the styling and layout. Learn more at W3Schools CSS

JavaScript

JavaScript adds interactivity. Learn more at W3Schools JavaScript

Frontend (React)

React.js is a popular JavaScript library used for building front-end user interfaces with reusable components.

Why React?

  • Reusable components
  • Virtual DOM for fast updates
  • Large ecosystem and community

Setup

npx create-react-app my-app
cd my-app
npm start

Resources

  • Official React Documentation
  • React Tutorial Video

Backend (Node.js)

Node.js allows you to run JavaScript on the server side.

Getting Started

npm init -y
npm install express

Resources

  • Node.js Official Site
  • Express.js Documentation

Full Stack (Django)

Django is a high-level Python web framework for rapid development.

Getting Started

pip install django
django-admin startproject myproject
cd myproject
python manage.py runserver

Resources

  • Django Official Site

Data Science

Learn data analysis, visualization, and machine learning with Python.

Key Libraries

  • NumPy - numerical computing
  • Pandas - data manipulation
  • Matplotlib/Seaborn - visualization
  • Scikit-learn - machine learning

Resources

  • Kaggle Learn

Computing

Learn about CPUs, GPUs, and computing resources for data-intensive tasks.

Topics

  • Parallel computing
  • GPU programming with CUDA
  • Cloud computing (AWS, GCP, Azure)

Figma

Learn how to design your website!

Table of Contents

  1. Building your moodboard with Pinterest
  2. Figuring out your fonts and colors
  3. Using Figma to build out your designs
  4. Importing your designs into code

Resources

  • Pinterest for Moodboards
  • Adobe Fonts
  • Coolors Color Palette Generator
  • Figma
  • Figma Tutorial (43 mins)
